paper_title: Mitigating agricultural fires with carrot or stick? Evidence from China
notes: Data prepared by results/2303/prep_data_2303.R from biomass_plant_year_month_MCD12Q1.dta + plant_weather.dta.
Outcome: ft90 — fire frequency count within 0-90 km radius of BPP (main Table 2 outcome).
Treatment: tbp = 1 if biomass power plant is operational in year-month. Binary, absorbing, staggered.
Time variable: ym = (year-2001)*12 + month. Monthly data 2001m1 to 2019m12 (228 periods).
gvar_CS: entry month of BPP operation (125 unique cohorts). Never-treated = 0 (487 plants).
TWFE FE override: id^month (plant×calendar month) + prov^year^month (province×year×month).
Paper uses Conley spatial SE (600km bandwidth). Template uses cluster by plant id.
Table 2 Col 3: paper reports -4.863 (1.780 Conley). R TWFE: -4.836 (1.495 cluster). Coefficient ~0.6% gap.
CS-DID uses simple unit+time FEs (no interaction FEs). Weather controls NOT in cs_controls (Pattern 42).
SA uses sunab(gvar_CS, ym). Gardner uses did2s with same FE structure.
Event study: es_2303.R replicates Fig. 4 (k=10) with TWFE, CS-NT, SA, Gardner.
Event study uses YEARLY event time: year_month = first treated year per (id, month) pair.
Paper uses did_imputation (Borusyak 2022). Gardner replicates this; pre-trends flat (~0).
CS-NT diverges at late horizons (Pattern 38: shrinking not-yet-treated control).
TWFE and SA converge well; all estimators confirm significant negative post-treatment effect.

AUDIT 2026-04-19: Paper-auditor EXACT (0.01% vs paper -4.836). Full pipeline re-run 2026-04-19: TWFE no-ctrls (Spec B) = -5.075 (previously NA — now recovered); CS-NT with 7 weather ctrls (Spec A): simple=-2.037, dynamic=-1.709 (previously FAIL_memory — now recovered). Pipeline took ~2.5 hours on 217k obs × 7 ctrls × 125 cohorts doubly-robust CS-DID. All estimators confirm negative effect; controls attenuate CS-NT magnitude by ~60-70% (weather strongly predictive). 5-estimator direction unanimous.
